prompt: Two interfaces provide conflicting default methods and the class does not resolve the ambiguity. What is the bug?
choices: A class can only implement one interface at a time
Left and Right both provide a default tag() method; Both inherits two conflicting defaults and must override tag() to resolve the ambiguity — the compiler requires this
default methods are inherited from the leftmost interface in the implements list
The compiler automatically picks the alphabetically first interface's default
explanation: When two unrelated interfaces provide a default method with the same signature, the implementing class must override it to resolve the conflict. The compiler reports 'class Both inherits unrelated defaults for tag()'. Fix: add public String tag() { return Left.super.tag(); } or whatever resolution is desired.
